methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $289M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $2.3B.

private.name: Exotec
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series D), post-money $2B

data.narrative:
Solinftec, a Series D robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $289M to date, is evaluated against a comparable universe of four same-sector peers spanning Series C to E, supplemented by a broader set of eight private firms and one public reference point. The peer median valuation stands at $1.8B, with a range of $1.0B (Nimble Robotics, Series C) to $2.3B (Divergent, Series E).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 1.5x multiplier typical of Series D to next-stage transitions, implies $434M. Peer-median methodology—anchored to the four sampled competitors (Exotec at $2.0B in Series D, Hadrian at $1.6B in Series C, Divergent at $2.3B in Series E, and Nimble Robotics at $1.0B in Series C)—implies $1.8B. The arithmetic median of these two approaches yields $1.1B, with a low bound of $368M and a high bound of $2.1B.

The wide spread between methods reflects tension between capital-deployment benchmarks and peer-set positioning. The peer-median method carries greater weight given Solinftec's positioning relative to late-stage robotics companies, though stage inflation and revenue divergence among comparables introduce material uncertainty. A valuation range of $1.0B to $1.8B appears most defensible pending revenue and margin visibility.
